title: Exam Week: My Nervous System's Least Favorite Week

sentences.en: Monday. This week is exam week, which means my textbook and workbook have basically become permanent parts of my body.
sentences.zh: 星期一。這禮拜是考試週，代表我的課本和作業本基本上已經變成我身體的一部分了。

sentences.en: Our teacher wrote a full review schedule on the blackboard, using so much chalk that her hands turned completely white by the end.
sentences.zh: 老師在黑板上寫了一整份複習進度，用掉的粉筆多到她的手到最後整個變成白色的。

sentences.en: "A little pressure before an exam is normal," she said. "Too much pressure, though, and your brain forgets things it actually knows."
sentences.zh: 「考試前有一點壓力是正常的，」她說：「但壓力太大，你的腦子反而會忘記自己明明知道的東西。」

sentences.en: Bernice claims she handles exam stress by staying extremely calm, which is funny, since she also color-codes her notes at midnight in a mild panic.
sentences.zh: Bernice 宣稱她應付考試壓力的方式是保持超級冷靜，這很好笑，因為她也會在半夜有點恐慌地把筆記分色標記。

sentences.en: Our first exam was a spelling test, and I felt fairly confident until the very last word completely erased itself from my memory.
sentences.zh: 我們第一場考試是拼字測驗，我一直覺得滿有把握的，直到最後一個字整個從我記憶裡消失。

sentences.en: I stared at the blank line so long that I am fairly sure the word itself got nervous watching me.
sentences.zh: 我盯著那條空白線盯了好久，久到我幾乎確定那個單字自己都被我盯得緊張起來了。

sentences.en: One boy in our class got caught trying to cheat by writing tiny notes on his hand, which the teacher noticed almost immediately.
sentences.zh: 班上有個男生想靠在手上寫小抄作弊，結果幾乎馬上就被老師發現了。

sentences.en: "Cheating gives you a fake result," she told him afterward. "It does not give you real knowledge, and eventually that catches up with you."
sentences.zh: 「作弊只會給你一個假的分數，」她事後跟他說：「並不會給你真正的知識，這件事遲早會找上你。」

sentences.en: Our math exam followed, using the same standard as every other exam this year: fair questions, but absolutely no room for careless mistakes.
sentences.zh: 接著是數學考試，跟今年其他每一場考試用的是同一套標準：題目公平，但完全沒有粗心犯錯的空間。

sentences.en: I finished with just enough time to review my answers once, catching one small mistake that would have counted as a full failure on that question.
sentences.zh: 我剛好留了足夠的時間把答案複習一遍，抓到了一個小錯誤，那個錯誤原本會讓那一題整題算失敗。

sentences.en: By Wednesday, exam stress had turned our whole classroom quiet in a way that felt almost unnatural for a room full of thirteen-year-olds.
sentences.zh: 到了星期三，考試的壓力讓我們整間教室安靜得幾乎不像是一間坐滿十三歲小孩的教室。

sentences.en: William, meanwhile, breezed through his own small quiz at school and came home talking about it like he had won a national competition.
sentences.zh: 同時，William 在學校輕輕鬆鬆考完他自己的小考，回家講起這件事的樣子，好像他剛贏得一場全國性的比賽。

sentences.en: "Success feels the same no matter how big the test is," he announced, which was actually a surprisingly wise thing for an eight-year-old to say.
sentences.zh: 他宣布：「不管考試有多大，成功的感覺都一樣，」對一個八歲小孩來說，這句話出乎意料地有智慧。

sentences.en: By Thursday's science exam, I had accepted that some questions I simply would not know, and that accepting this calmly was better than panicking.
sentences.zh: 到了星期四的理化考試，我已經接受有些題目我就是不會，而且平靜接受這件事，比恐慌好多了。

sentences.en: Our teacher reminded us before handing out the papers that failure on one exam does not define an entire semester, let alone an entire person.
sentences.zh: 發考卷之前，老師提醒我們，一次考試失敗，不代表整個學期，更不代表整個人。

sentences.en: By Friday afternoon, exam week officially ended, and the whole class let out a group sigh so loud it practically had its own echo.
sentences.zh: 到了星期五下午，考試週正式結束，全班一起發出的嘆氣聲大到幾乎自己有了回音。

sentences.en: Bernice and I agreed that surviving exam week successfully deserved its own small celebration, even if our actual scores were still a mystery.
sentences.zh: Bernice 和我都同意，成功撐過考試週值得自己小小慶祝一下，就算我們真正的分數目前還是個謎。

sentences.en: Lesson of the day: exam week is stressful, but somehow surviving it with your confidence mostly intact feels like a success all on its own.
sentences.zh: 今日心得：考試週很有壓力，但不知怎麼地，能帶著大致完好的自信撐過去，本身就算是一種成功。

quiz.question: What happens to the boy who tries to cheat during the spelling test?
options: He gets away with it completely.
He is sent to another classroom.

quiz.question: What does the teacher say about failure on one exam?
options: It does not define an entire semester or person.
It means you must repeat the whole year.
It always means you did not study.
It should be reported to your parents immediately.

quiz.question: What does William say about success after his own small quiz?
options: It feels the same no matter how big the test is.
It only counts if you get a perfect score.
It is only real if adults notice it.
It does not matter at his age.
